sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position after UP
seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

for i in range(30, min(43, len(ascii_lines))):
    print(f"  row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][8:28]}")

print("\n=== Player object ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 18:
        bnd = n['boundary']
        print(f"  id={n['id']} bnd={bnd}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The player moved UP by 6 rows (from row 36-42 to row 30-36). The trail (w/g pattern) moved with the player. The old player position now shows the gray wall pattern (G/g). So:
1. Arrow keys move the player character
2. The trail follows the player
3. The player moved up 6 rows in one UP action
Now I understand the game better. The player can move around the room. The goal is likely to move the player to touch/interact with the colored blocks on the right side in the order shown in the bottom panel: Red → Green → Blue.
